description_long: This house features a complex, multi-tiered wooden structure with a prominent, steeply pitched gable roof at its center, surrounded by several lower, shingled roof sections. The facade combines a white lower story with timber-framed upper stories, giving it a rustic, historical appearance. Windows are arranged in symmetrical rows, with small, multi-pane designs typical of traditional European architecture. The main entryway is marked by a double wooden door at the front, accessible via a short, orange-tiled porch that stands out against the otherwise muted color palette. The building's massing suggests a spacious interior, possibly with multiple rooms and levels. Decorative wooden trim and overhanging eaves add to the architectural detail and visual interest.
description: A large, multi-story timber house with a steep gable roof, white lower walls, and symmetrical window rows. The front features a double wooden door and a distinctive orange-tiled porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with double doors, orange-tiled porch, and symmetrical windows on the white lower story.
description_view_1: Rear view dominated by the expansive shingled roof and timber walls, with minimal window openings and no visible entry.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the tall central gable, large wooden wall sections, and a few upper-story windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the continuation of the white lower story, timber upper story, and the layered roof structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Double wooden door with a short orange-tiled porch, no steps, and minimal decorative elements.
